input.prompt: Goal: stand up the nightly warehouse export job. Acceptance criteria: every output file validates against the warehouse table schema; turning the job on in production requires data-team sign-off.

metadata.criterion: Schema and sign-off criteria each get the right gate.

input.prompt: Goal: write a URL slugifier. Acceptance criteria: unicode, emoji, and mixed-case inputs produce stable ASCII slugs, demonstrated by unit tests.

metadata.criterion: Unit-testable behavior gets a unit_test gate.

input.prompt: Goal: improve the support bot. Acceptance criteria: each week the bot's answers are scored against a quality rubric by an LLM judge.

metadata.criterion: LLM-judge scoring maps to an eval gate.

input.prompt: Goal: migrate user avatars to the new storage bucket. Acceptance criteria: 1) every existing avatar URL keeps working; 2) new uploads land in the new bucket; 3) the old bucket becomes read-only after the migration completes.

metadata.criterion: The gate matrix covers every extracted criterion, checked by a deterministic parity node.
